content.text:
**Short answer:** Yes, but only a small set today — not the new agent vars.

Cloud Run runtime env is wired in two places in the scaffold:

1. **`deploy-cloudrun.workflow.yml`** — on each deploy sets `AUTH_EMAIL_ENABLED` / `AUTH_GOOGLE_ENABLED` plus Secret Manager refs for `DATABASE_URL` and `SUPABASE_URL`.
2. **`wire-cloudrun-supabase.sh`** — when `REDEPLOY_CLOUD_RUN=1` and the service already exists, updates the same auth env vars via `gcloud run services update`.

It did **not** set `AGENTS_MCP_HTTP_URL` or `AGENTS_SKILLS_REPO_URL` before.

---

**What I added:**

| Var | Value | Where |
|-----|-------|--------|
| `AGENTS_SKILLS_REPO_URL` | Normalized `target_repo_url` from bootstrap | GitHub production var + Cloud Run env |
| `AGENTS_MCP_HTTP_URL` | `{cloud_run_url}/api/v1/mcp` | Cloud Run env (after deploy / redeploy) |

**Files changed:**
- `wire-cloudrun-supabase.sh` — sets `AGENTS_SKILLS_REPO_URL` GitHub var; includes both agent vars on redeploy
- `deploy-cloudrun.workflow.yml` — sets `AGENTS_SKILLS_REPO_URL` on deploy, then patches `AGENTS_MCP_HTTP_URL` from the live service URL
- `setup-cloudrun-github-main.sh` — exports `TARGET_REPO_URL` into the wire script
- `print-publish-link-summary.sh` — operator instructions right after the Supabase site URL block

The summary now prints something like:

```
Cloud Run agent env (set on the service if deploy did not wire them)
  AGENTS_SKILLS_REPO_URL=https://github.com/org/repo
  AGENTS_MCP_HTTP_URL=https://….a.run.app/api/v1/mcp
```

Existing repos that already deployed will pick up the new vars on the next `deploy-cloudrun.yml` run, or via `REDEPLOY_CLOUD_RUN=1` through the wire script.

[REDACTED]

EOF
)"

input.command:
cd /workspaces/agno && git push -u origin HEAD && gh pr create --title "feat(provisioner): wire agent MCP and skills repo env on Cloud Run" --body "$(cat <<'EOF'
## Summary
- Set `AGENTS_SKILLS_REPO_URL` (normalized GitHub repo URL) as a production GitHub variable and Cloud Run env var during bootstrap wiring and deploy.
- Set `AGENTS_MCP_HTTP_URL` to `{cloud_run_url}/api/v1/mcp` after each Cloud Run deploy and on redeploy.
- Add operator instructions in the publish/link summary next to the Supabase site URL guidance.

## Test plan
- [ ] Bootstrap a scaffold repo and confirm `AGENTS_SKILLS_REPO_URL` appears in GitHub production environment vars.
- [ ] Run `deploy-cloudrun.yml` and verify Cloud Run service env includes `AGENTS_SKILLS_REPO_URL` and `AGENTS_MCP_HTTP_URL`.
- [ ] Confirm `print-publish-link-summary.sh` prints suggested agent env values after deploy.

EOF
)"

# Babysit PR
Your job is to get this PR to a merge-ready state.

Check PR status, comments, and latest CI and resolve any issues until the PR is ready to merge.

1. Merge conflicts: Intelligently resolve any merge conflicts, preserving the intent and correctness of changes on your branch and the base branch. If intents conflict, abort the merge and ask for clarification.
2. Comments: Review active unresolved comments (including Bugbot) and resolve change requests / bug reports where valid. When fetching GitHub comments, filter out resolved threads first. Read only each comment body and the minimum location/URL needed to act on it; do not read the entire JSON output or other unnecessary payload data. Carefully validate issues reported by Bugbot and only take action on those that are valid; explain when you disagree or are unsure.
3. CI: Fix CI issues caused by changes within this PR's scope. Never change CI checks/workflows just to make failures pass, or make unrelated code changes; if that would be required, report back instead. For merge-blocking failures that seem unrelated to this PR, check whether the branch is behind the base branch and merge latest changes, since another PR may have fixed them. Push scoped fixes and re-watch CI until mergeable + green + comments triaged.

SERVICE_URL="$(
            gcloud run services describe "$GCP_CLOUD_RUN_SERVICE" \
              --region "$GCP_REGION" \
              --format='value(status.url)'
          )"
          if [[ -z "$SERVICE_URL" ]]; then
            echo "Cloud Run service URL is empty after deploy; cannot set AGENTS_MCP_HTTP_URL." >&2
            exit 1
          fi
          gcloud run services update "$GCP_CLOUD_RUN_SERVICE" \
            --region "$GCP_REGION" \
            --update-env-vars "AGENTS_MCP_HTTP_URL=${SERVICE_URL%/}/api/v1/mcp"

PR [#236](https://github.com/trilogy-group/ab-agno/pull/236) is merge-ready.

**Status**
- Mergeable, no conflicts (`CLEAN`)
- CI green: lint, type, test-unit, conventional-pr-title, Semgrep

**Greptile review — addressed**
1. **P1 (valid):** Deploy now fails if `SERVICE_URL` is empty instead of writing a broken `AGENTS_MCP_HTTP_URL=/api/v1/mcp`.
2. **P2 (valid):** Shared `normalize-github-repo-url.mjs` replaces duplicated inline Node blocks in `wire-cloudrun-supabase.sh` and `print-publish-link-summary.sh`.

**Pushed:** `64421a9` — `fix(provisioner): guard MCP URL deploy and dedupe repo URL helper`

Greptile hasn’t re-run on the new commit yet; both findings are fixed in scope. Ready to merge when you are.

<user_query>
have we introduced any new env vars to be added to this app
</user_query>

Checking whether the new vars are consumed by this repo's app or only wired into the scaffold/Cloud Run bootstrap.

[REDACTED]

No — not for **this** app (AgentOS / `ab-agno`).

The PR only adds two env vars for **provisioned customer apps** on Cloud Run, via the scaffold bootstrap:

| Var | Set on | Read by AgentOS? |
|-----|--------|------------------|
| `AGENTS_SKILLS_REPO_URL` | Cloud Run + GitHub production var | No |
| `AGENTS_MCP_HTTP_URL` | Cloud Run only | No |

They appear only under `agents/provisioner/fixtures/rebase-ai-scaffold/` (wire script, deploy workflow, publish summary). Nothing in `app/`, `src/`, `compose.yaml`, or `example.env` references them.

**For AgentOS:** nothing to add to `.env` or compose.

**For scaffolded repos:** bootstrap sets them automatically. Operators only need to verify manually if deploy didn’t wire them (the publish summary prints suggested values).

If those vars are meant to be consumed by the **customer app** runtime (e.g. Cursor agents in the scaffold), that wiring would live in that app’s code — not in this repo yet. Right now we only **inject** them at deploy time.

[REDACTED]
